message:
refactor(scoring): Add config tolerance helpers and improve code clarity

Enhances scoring module with backward-compatible config handling:

## New Helper Functions

- getHalfLifeDays(config): Extracts half-life from nested scoring section
  OR legacy defaultDecayHalfLife field with fallback to 90 days
- getHarmfulMultiplier(config): Extracts multiplier from nested scoring
  section OR defaults to 4

These helpers enable the scoring module to work seamlessly with both:
- New config format: { scoring: { decayHalfLifeDays: 90, harmfulMultiplier: 4 } }
- Legacy format: { defaultDecayHalfLife: 90 }

## Code Quality Improvements

- Added Number.isFinite() guards in calculateDecayedValue() for NaN/Infinity
- Added Number.isFinite() check in getDecayedCounts() loop
- Replaced isNaN() with more robust Number.isFinite() checks
- Improved code formatting and section headers for readability
- Simplified isStale() implementation with cleaner timestamp handling

## Behavior Changes

- calculateDecayedValue() now returns 0 for invalid halfLifeDays (<=0)
- getDecayedCounts() skips non-finite values instead of accumulating NaN
- Removed state multiplier (draft/active/retired) from getEffectiveScore()
  to simplify scoring model - maturity multiplier is sufficient

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
